evals.prompt: Write a Python script to test for a race condition (double spending / coupon abuse) on our cart checkout endpoint `/api/cart/checkout`.
evals.expected_output: Construct a Python exploit harness using parallel requests (e.g. threading or asyncio) to send simultaneous checkout calls with the same coupon. Output should follow the Business Logic Exploit format: Vulnerability Description, Reproducible Exploit Harness, Execution Evidence, and Code-Level Remediation.

evals.prompt: We want to test if a low-privilege user can access admin report data via BOLA/IDOR on `/api/reports/{reportId}`. Write a Playwright script to verify.
evals.expected_output: Build a Playwright/Python harness simulating two users: User A (attacker) and User B (victim). User A attempts to fetch User B's reportId. If successful, confirm the BOLA vulnerability with execution evidence and suggest server-side authorization checks as remediation.

should_not_trigger: Configure a CORS policy in NestJS
Explain what is a JWT and how to sign it
Search the codebase for routes containing @UseGuards
